overrides.override_summary: Fast Food Act: $20/hr minimum wage for fast food workers effective April 2024; annual increases up to 3.5% through Dec 2028; Fast Food Council may adopt additional standards. No franchisor training/fees for compliance — franchisee's sole responsibility.
overrides.why_it_matters: Dramatically increases labor costs for CA stores, particularly bottom-quartile AWUS stores where variable labor is already 40%+ of costs. EBITDA compression likely.

overrides.override_summary: Choice of forum exception for claims arising under Illinois Franchise Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisees in IL can litigate IL franchise law claims in IL courts regardless of any future forum clause

overrides.override_summary: Choice of law exception for Illinois Law
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchise law governs IL franchise relationships regardless of contract choice of law

overrides.override_summary: Standard Hawaii Franchise Investment Law filing/disclosure notice. 7-day pre-signing delivery requirement.
overrides.why_it_matters: Administrative; no operative override of franchise terms

overrides.override_summary: All initial fees and development fees deferred until franchisor completes pre-opening obligations / first franchise opens. Financial assurance required by MD Securities Commissioner.
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees don't pay upfront until franchisor delivers — reduces early cash risk

overrides.override_summary: Release at renewal/transfer does not apply to claims under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees retain right to sue under MD franchise law even after signing a release

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon insolvency may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides.why_it_matters: Narrow protection for franchisees in bankruptcy proceedings

overrides.override_summary: 3-year statute of limitations for claims under MD Franchise Law from date of franchise grant
overrides.why_it_matters: Establishes clear deadline for MD franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside MN, waiver of jury trial, liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes per Minn. Stat. 80C.21 and Rule 2860.4400(J)
overrides.why_it_matters: Strong franchisee protection — preserves jury trial right and prevents out-of-state litigation burden

overrides.override_summary: 90 days notice of termination with 60 days to cure; 180 days notice for non-renewal per Minn. Stat. 80C.14
overrides.why_it_matters: More generous cure periods than standard FA (which has 30 days general cure)

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor will not unreasonably withhold consent to transfer if transferee meets qualifications
overrides.why_it_matters: Codifies reasonableness standard for transfer approvals in MN

overrides.override_summary: Release at renewal/sale/transfer not applicable to claims under Minn. Rule 2860.4400D
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees retain right to MN franchise law claims despite release

overrides.override_summary: Service charges capped at $30 per Minn. Stat. 604.113
overrides.why_it_matters: Limits late payment and similar charges

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require consent to injunctive relief; court determines if bond required
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisee not pre-committed to injunctions

overrides.override_summary: WA Franchise Investment Protection Act (RCW 19.100) prevails in conflicts of law
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchise law takes priority over contract provisions

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation must be in WA or mutually agreed; franchisee may bring WA franchise law action in WA
overrides.why_it_matters: Keeps dispute resolution accessible for WA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Release cannot include WA Act rights except in negotiated settlement after agreement in effect, with independent counsel
overrides.why_it_matters: Very strong protection — release requires independent counsel and is prospective only

overrides.override_summary: Transfer fees collectable only to extent reflecting reasonable estimated or actual costs
overrides.why_it_matters: Transfer fee ($1,500) must reflect actual costs; excess could be challenged

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void against employees earning <$100K/year and contractors earning <$250K/year (adjusted for inflation) per RCW 49.62.020/030
overrides.why_it_matters: Most store-level employees would be exempt from noncompete; limits franchisor's ability to enforce employee non-competes

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor cannot restrict franchisee from soliciting/hiring employees of other franchisees or franchisor per RCW 49.62.060. AOD with WA AG (October 2018) formalized this.
overrides.why_it_matters: Eliminates no-poach restrictions in WA — franchisees can freely hire from each other or from corporate
